messages.content:
edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: I ask, “Even today, might scientists make huge
mistakes?”
Gabriel says, “Of course, this happens every time. As
an example, what is famous as Expansion Theory related to
Georges Henri Joseph Edouard Lemaitre and (Edwin
Powell Hubble 1889-1953. Now, man knows about the
structures of atoms as the smallest unique structure of
elements. Electrons and planets are orbiting around their
cores or suns, and also the solar systems orbiting around the
black holes or the mighty gigantic stars of the galaxies. So,
how is it possible that the galaxies are an exception to the
rule of circling around the pole or the core of the cosmos,
and they are unleashed expanding?”

I ask, "Could scientists still make significant mistakes in the present day?"

Gabriel responds, "Absolutely, it happens more often than not. Take the famous Expansion Theory associated with Georges Henri Joseph Edouard Lemaitre and Edwin Powell Hubble. We now understand that atoms serve as the smallest distinct structures of elements, with electrons and planets orbiting around their cores or suns. Furthermore, solar systems revolve around black holes or colossal stars within galaxies. So, why then do galaxies deviate from the norm of circling around the center or core of the cosmos? How can they break free and expand?"

(Note: The edited excerpt eliminates unnecessary repetition and simplifies the language to enhance its readability.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Carol says, “It isn’t easy to accept and replace Gabriel’s
simple explanation with the theory of expansion, which
every astrophysicist in the world considers it as a fact.”
However, it is quite clear to me what Gabriel said. Because
I was one of the billions, who had the opportunity to find
out about galaxies that are orbiting around the pole.

Carol adds, "It's difficult to let go of Gabriel's straightforward explanation and embrace the theory of expansion, especially since every astrophysicist worldwide accepts it as fact."

However, Gabriel's explanation resonates with me. I too have been one of the countless individuals who have witnessed galaxies orbiting around the celestial pole.

(Note: The edited excerpt improves the flow and clarity of the sentences while maintaining the essence of the original passage.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Gabriel continues, “Many subjects like the Theory of
Expansion have not been explored precisely, by the elites
today.
“As you have heard it many times, that some people
believe in science only, and some are religious believers,
although those who believe in science are closer to the
realities of life and God than those who believe in holy
books. Actually, there is no contradiction in essence
between science and true religions.
“I like to remind you of the history of science and how
it developed.
“Religion came first, then philosophy, logic, and then
sciences. So if you take the prophets as a visionary, you see
that even basic scientific exploration needs inspiration or
perspective.”

Gabriel continues, "Many topics, such as the Theory of Expansion, remain poorly explored by the intellectual elites of our time."

"As you've undoubtedly heard before, there are individuals who solely rely on science, while others cling to their religious beliefs. Yet, those who place their faith in science often find themselves closer to the true nature of reality and the divine than those who follow holy texts. In truth, there is no inherent contradiction between science and genuine religions."

"Allow me to take you on a journey through the history of scientific development."

"Religion emerged first, followed by philosophy, logic, and eventually the sciences. If we consider prophets as visionaries, it becomes clear that even the most fundamental scientific pursuits require inspiration and perspective."

(Note: The edited excerpt enhances the fluidity and clarity of the sentences while maintaining the original content.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: At this time, the car stops in front of the restaurant.
The consul is a chubby and older man who loves Persia,
especially Shiraz and Persepolis.
He eagerly asks us, “How has your journey been so
far?” And he does not wait for our answers and asks again,
“How did you find Persepolis? Great. Did you see the Great
Gate of Nations and the Hall of Representatives of different
nations? The Achaemenes were the first empire in history
to establish a federative government, while The Greeks
formed the first democratic government.”

As the car comes to a halt in front of the restaurant, I notice the consul, a portly and seasoned gentleman with a fondness for Persia, particularly Shiraz and Persepolis.

Eagerly, he inquires, "How was your journey thus far?" without pausing for our response. He continues, "And how did you find Persepolis? Magnificent, I presume. Did you explore the awe-inspiring Great Gate of Nations and the Hall of Representatives, where countless nations convened? It's remarkable to think that the Achaemenes established the first federative government in history, while the Greeks were pioneers in developing democratic governance."

(Note: The edited excerpt enhances readability and flow while maintaining the original information provided.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Carol loves the consul and says, “You look more like an
academic than a politician.”
The consul does not consider it an offense and says,
“Your guess is absolutely correct.”
“I was teaching Middle Eastern history at the University
of Washington when the Ambassador and the Ministry of
State asked me to take the job as a cultural consultant at the
Tehran embassy and now the consul in Shiraz.”

Carol admires the consul and remarks, "You exude more of an academic aura rather than that of a politician."

The consul doesn't take offense and responds, "Your observation is absolutely on point."

"I had been teaching Middle Eastern history at the University of Washington when the Ambassador and the Ministry of State approached me. They asked me to serve as a cultural consultant at the Tehran embassy, and now, here I am, the consul in Shiraz."

(Note: The edited excerpt improves the flow and clarity of the sentences while maintaining the essence of the original passage.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: The tasty and colourful food is more appetizing with
sweet talks of the Consul about Shiraz and its great
celebrities whose mausoleums are there as; Hafiz, Saadi,
and many others.
He brings out academic references and archeological
facts for any word that he has been speaking about, which
makes his talks more valuable to listeners. Carol and I are
so fond of the Consul and his sweet speech that we eat more
food than our limits. When our stomachs started to growl
and ache, then we realized that we ate too much.
Finally, we appreciate the Consul for his warm
hospitality and ask his permission, then we leave the
restaurant and heading toward our hotel in the vicinity of
Persepolis. When we get into our suite at the hotel, we find
out that the experienced nurse has taken the necessary care
of our child. And now both are sleeping, one in his bed and
the nurse on the couch. When we enter the room, the nurse
quickly gets up and says, “The embassy messenger, who did
not want to disturb you during lunch, sent the itinerary to
your email address.”

The delectable and vibrant dishes become even more irresistible as the Consul paints a vivid picture of Shiraz and its illustrious luminaries, such as Hafiz and Saadi, whose mausoleums grace the city.

With each word he utters, the Consul supplements his narrative with academic references and archaeological insights, adding immense value to his captivating discourse. Carol and I are so entranced by his eloquence that we indulge in more food than our stomachs can handle. It is only when our bellies start to protest and ache that we realize we have exceeded our limits.

Expressing our deep gratitude for the Consul's warm hospitality, we ask for permission to leave the restaurant and make our way to the hotel in the vicinity of Persepolis. As we enter the luxurious suite, we discover that the experienced nurse has diligently cared for our child. Both the child and the nurse are now sound asleep, one in their bed and the other on the couch. The nurse promptly rises upon our arrival and informs us, "The embassy messenger, mindful of your lunch, sent the itinerary to your email address."

(Note: The edited excerpt improves the overall flow, clarity, and cohesion of the passage.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Early morning, we are all on our way to the North East
of Persia in our private wagon.
After a few hours, the train reaches northeast of Persia,
Mashhad, and we have to change course and travel a few
hundred miles to the southeast. We take the opportunity and
visit a few historical places and museums in the city of
Mashhad.
After a few hours, our wagon is ready, and we are
scheduled to head to what was once a part of the vast sea
after The Great Flood and then a significant earthquake, that
caused a massive desert about 15,000 years ago. And now,
with the construction of the canal and the bringing of water
and its desalination from the Caspian Sea, has flourished.
Bringing water to the desert has made it smaller, and a tiny
part of the desert remained today. And the rest of the great
desert has been occupied by lakes, farms, promenades, and
newly built cities.

In the early morning, we embark on our journey to the northeast of Persia in our private wagon. After a few hours, the train arrives at Mashhad, located in the northeastern region of Persia. However, our intended destination lies a few hundred miles to the southeast. Seizing the opportunity, we explore the rich historical sites and museums scattered throughout the city of Mashhad.

Once our wagon is prepared, we embark on our scheduled route towards a place that was once engulfed by a vast sea before The Great Flood and a cataclysmic earthquake transformed it into a massive desert approximately 15,000 years ago. However, with the creation of a canal and the importation of water, as well as its desalination from the Caspian Sea, this once desolate desert has experienced a remarkable resurgence. The introduction of irrigation has caused it to shrink, leaving only a small oasis behind. The remaining expansive desert has been reclaimed, giving rise to lakes, farmlands, serene promenades, and newly established cities.

(Note: The edited excerpt refines the language and enhances the overall readability and coherence of the passage.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Carol says, “All the information has been written in the
tourist bulletin. And it says in here that the authorities are
sweetening the water with a system that is called absorbing
and filtration plus solar energy, and it is entirely
environmentally friendly technology and consumes the
least fossil fuel or electricity.”

Carol informs us, "All the pertinent information is outlined in the tourist bulletin. According to this brochure, the authorities have implemented a water purification system known as 'absorption and filtration plus solar energy.' This innovative technology not only sweetens the water but also operates in complete harmony with the environment, minimizing the consumption of fossil fuels and electricity."

(Note: The edited excerpt ensures clarity and conciseness while retaining the vital details provided in the original passage.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: After passing through woods, resorts and many
beautiful lakes, finally we reach to a small city in the center
of a desert.
As soon as the train stops and our wagon is detached,
one of the embassy’s local employees receives us kindly
and takes us to the only hotel in the town. The guy says,
“The Embassy has paid for your staying here, and if you
agree, I will come tomorrow at 8.00 a.m. to accompany you
to a place, which is the purpose of your trip to this desert
and town.” And after getting our consents, the guy says,
“Good day,” and leaves us.

After traversing dense forests, charming resorts, and numerous picturesque lakes, we finally arrive at a small city nestled in the heart of a sprawling desert.

As the train halts and our wagon is detached, a friendly local employee from the embassy warmly welcomes us and guides us to the solitary hotel in town. With a gracious smile, he conveys, "The Embassy has graciously arranged and covered your accommodation here. If you agree, I will rendezvous with you tomorrow at 8.00 a.m. to accompany you to the main destination of your desert expedition." Upon receiving our consent, he bids us farewell, saying, "Have a pleasant day," and departs.

(Note: The edited excerpt enhances the flow and readability of the passage.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Carol and I don’t know what kind of big surprise we are
about to have tomorrow. And what makes this part of the
trip different and distinctive from other places and cities
we’ve ever visited. The only thing that attracts our attention
is the massive mustache of the Persian employee of the
embassy.
Our hotel and obviously the suite is not as luxurious as
the four star hotels we have stayed in up to now, but
surprisingly it is obsessively clean and organized. We go
downstairs to the lobby, and from there to the restaurant, we
witness the same cleaned and organized facilities all around
the establishment.

Carol and I find ourselves on the precipice of a remarkable surprise awaiting us tomorrow, one that promises to set this leg of our journey apart from any other city or place we have ever visited. Curiously, the only conspicuous detail that captures our attention is the immense mustache sported by the Persian employee from the embassy.

Although our hotel and certainly our suite lack the opulence of the four-star establishments we have previously stayed in, we are pleasantly surprised by the obsessive cleanliness and meticulous organization. Venturing downstairs to the lobby, and then proceeding to the restaurant, we are greeted by the same impeccably maintained and efficiently arranged facilities throughout the entire establishment.

(Note: The edited excerpt improves the flow and harmony of the sentences.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: At the same time, Carol and I say together, “Whoa,
everything is shining in here.” And the waitress brings our
orders, and we find the food here as good as the fancy
restaurants, and the quality is the same.
Finally, Carol could not resist her curiosity and asks the
manager, “What is your secret to managing this restaurant
so clean and tidy?”
The manager in fluent English says, “All the staffs are
Muslims and Sophists and residents of this town.
“One of our tenets is that hygiene and tidiness come
from the faith, and the other is that caring of guests is as
precious as prays.”
I ask, “But this is not the same for other Muslims or
even Sophists in other countries.”
The manager answers, “Well, most Muslims,
Christians, Jews, and others have inherited their
religious beliefs from their parents, not by searching and
studying to choose a belief for themselves.”
We think his interpretation of religion is realistic and
logical.

In unison, Carol and I exclaim, "Wow, everything gleams with a brilliant shine in here." As the waitress promptly delivers our orders, we discover that the food not only matches the caliber of fancy restaurants but possesses the same undeniable quality.

Unable to contain her curiosity any longer, Carol leans in and asks the manager, "What is your secret to upholding such impeccable cleanliness and tidiness in this restaurant?"

In fluent English, the manager responds, "All of our staff members are both Muslims and Sophists, deeply rooted in this town. Our guiding principle is that cleanliness and tidiness stem from religious faith, and we hold the cherished belief that attending to our guests is as essential as offering prayers."

Curiosity piqued, I interject, "However, this perspective on faith and cleanliness is not necessarily shared among all Muslims or Sophists in different countries."

With a thoughtful nod, the manager replies, "Indeed, many individuals, regardless of their religious affiliation, have merely inherited their beliefs from their parents, rather than diligently searching and studying to choose their own path."

Appreciating the manager's realistic and logical interpretation of religion, we silently reflect upon his words.

(Note: The edited excerpt improves the flow and coherence of the passage while maintaining the essence of the original text.)

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: The next day, the local employee of the embassy comes
to us and says, “I want to take you to a place a few hundred
yards away, and if you don’t mind, we can get there on
foot.” Carol and I love walking and jogging, so we willingly
accept.
In addition to the ethics, kindness, and spirit of the
people we face hygiene and tidiness everywhere.
The employee says, “This underground aqueduct is
2500 years old. The source of the stream that you see now,
comes from the underground conduit consisting of many
wells that each is connected to another by a few hundred
tunnels. And the main and the deepest well, (Mother Well)
with 300 meters’ depth, is at the vicinity of the mountains,
and 100 miles away from here.”

The following day, the local embassy employee approaches us and proposes, "I would like to take you to a place just a few hundred yards away, and if you don't mind, we can reach it on foot." Given our fondness for walking and jogging, Carol and I readily agree to the suggestion.

Notably, apart from the prevailing ethos of kindness and the warm spirit we encounter, hygiene and tidiness permeate every aspect of this place.

The employee proceeds, "This underground aqueduct is an astonishing 2500 years old. The stream you currently see stems from an intricate network of interconnected wells, linked by a labyrinth of several hundred tunnels. The primary and deepest well, known as the 'Mother Well,' reaches a staggering depth of 300 meters. It is situated in the proximity of the mountains, approximately 100 miles away from here."

(Note: The edited excerpt improves the language flow and coherence while maintaining the original content.)
